fact: February 7 is exactly 38 days into the year, which means there are still 327 days left to go!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is prime time for spotting animal tracks in fresh snow - foxes, rabbits, and deer leave perfect paw prints like nature's own detective story.
Tree sap starts flowing upward around this time in maple trees across North America, even though it's still freezing cold outside!
The sun rises about 2 minutes earlier than it did just one week ago in most places - winter is slowly losing its grip on daylight.
February 7 falls smack in the middle of Aquarius season, making it one of the most 'Aquarian' days of the entire year.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere countries, this date marks peak summer heat - perfect beach weather while the north shivers!
Groundhogs are still deep in their winter sleep around this time, dreaming away in burrows up to 6 feet underground.
The moon phase on February 7 changes every year, but when it's a full moon on this date, it creates some of the brightest winter nights you'll ever see.
This date sits almost perfectly between the winter solstice and spring equinox - it's like the exact middle of winter's reign.
In many cold regions, February 7 marks when ice on ponds and lakes reaches its thickest point of the entire winter season.
Believe it or not, some brave flowers like snowdrops and winter aconites start poking through frozen ground around this time in milder climates.
The average temperature difference between February 7 in Alaska versus February 7 in Florida can be over 100 degrees Fahrenheit!
